qualifying_credentials: British Fantasy Award — Best Artist win
Society of Illustrators Gold or Silver Medal
National Portrait Gallery London — selected exhibition
exceptions: Exceptional gallery CV (Diego Andrade standard: MoCA, Soumaya Museum, Gallery Nucleus level)
exclusions: Universes Beyond-only artists (no non-UB MTG cards)
Nominations without wins (except NPG London and Communication Arts which are selection-based)

Donato Giancola.credentials: Hugo Award wins: 2006, 2007, 2009 (3 wins). Chesley Award wins: 1997, 1999, 2001, 2002, 2004, 2005, 2006, 2007, 2008, 2009, 2011, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017 (15+ wins). World Fantasy Award 2011. Spectrum Grandmaster 2019. Hamilton King Award.
Donato Giancola.notes: Stopped MTG work 2024 — supply permanently capped. Anchor artist.

Bob Eggleton.credentials: Hugo Award wins: 1994, 1996, 1997, 1998, 1999, 2001, 2003, 2004 (8 wins). Chesley Award wins: 12 wins including Lifetime Achievement 2012. Spectrum Grand Master.
Bob Eggleton.notes: Stopped MTG work 1998 — supply permanently capped. Godzilla/kaiju specialist.

Rebecca Guay.credentials: Spectrum Gold Medal (multiple). SOI Gold Medal. Museum of American Illustration.
Rebecca Guay.confidence_note: Spectrum and SOI years to be verified against primary sources

Omar Rayyan.credentials: Chesley Award wins: 2005 (Best Cover Magazine + Artistic Achievement), 2007 (Best Interior), 2012 (Best Color Work Unpublished + Lifetime Achievement), 2023 (Best Interior). Spectrum Gold Medal + Silver Medal.

Iris Compiet.credentials: Chesley Award win 2021 (Best Interior Illustration, Dark Crystal Bestiary). Jack Gaughan Award. Ippy Gold Medal.

Tran Nguyen.credentials: World Fantasy Award 2022. Chesley Award wins: 2016 (Best Cover Magazine), 2017 (Best Cover Hardcover). Spectrum Gold Medal x2. SOI medals.

Jeremy Wilson.credentials: Spectrum 24 Gold Medal. SOI-LA Gold Medal 2024. Star Wars/Lucasfilm credits.

Charles Urbach.credentials: Chesley Award wins: 2018 (Best Color Work), 2019 (Best Product Illustration), 2020 (Best Gaming-Related), 2021 (Best Cover Paperback). 4 Chesley wins total.

Ryan Yee.credentials: Chesley Award win 2016 (Best Gaming-Related Illustration, Fruit of the First Tree, Fate Reforged).

Kai Carpenter.credentials: Chesley Award win 2021 (Best Color Work Unpublished, The Selkie). RISD trained. Traditional oils.

David Palumbo.credentials: Chesley Award win 2013 (Best Gaming-Related Illustration, Ereshkigal Death Mistress). 3 Spectrum medals.
David Palumbo.notes: Also in database as Dave Palumbo.

J.P. Targete.credentials: Chesley Award win 2001 (Best Cover Illustration Paperback, Circle at Center by Douglas Niles).
J.P. Targete.notes: 27 MTG cards. Food Chain is top card ($28, Legacy staple).

Julie Benbassat.notes: 4 MTG cards, all Lorwyn Eclipsed 2025. Signs via MountainMage.

Elliot Lang.credentials: Communication Arts 2026 Annual (juried selection). SOI-LA Silver Medal.
Elliot Lang.notes: 4 MTG cards, all Streets of New Capenna 2022. Not framework Tier 1 via major award — Communication Arts qualifies under updated framework.

Erol Otus.credentials: OG MTG Lane exception — Alpha-era D&D artist, personal/historical significance.
Erol Otus.notes: Connected on Facebook. Kokusho SLD borderless in collection.

Diego Andrade.credentials: Framework exception — exceptional gallery CV: Gallery Nucleus, MoCA Bogotá, Soumaya Museum.
Diego Andrade.notes: Via Donny Caltrider. Now WotC MTG Art Director.

Rovina Cai.credentials: Hugo Award wins: 2021, 2022, 2024 (3 wins). World Fantasy Award wins: 2019, 2021 (2 wins).
Rovina Cai.notes: Shop drops only. No custom commissions. Signs via Mark Arnowitz. Highest acquisition priority.

Ryan Pancoast.credentials: Chesley Award win 2023 (Best Gaming-Related Illustration, Discover the Impossible, Kamigawa Neon Dynasty).
Ryan Pancoast.notes: 210+ MTG cards in oils. Active AP store. Top card: Cavern of Souls AVR ($28).

Lindsey Look.credentials: Chesley Award win 2021 (Best Gaming-Related Illustration, Sublime Epiphany, Core Set 2021).
Lindsey Look.notes: Traditional media. Signs via Mark Arnowitz.

Eric Wilkerson.credentials: Chesley Award win 2020 (Best Cover Illustration Hardcover, Tristan Strong Punches a Hole in the Sky).
Eric Wilkerson.notes: Painted AP not confirmed. Top card: Amulet of Vigor ($22).

Julie Dillon.credentials: Hugo Award wins: 2014, 2015, 2017 (3 wins). Chesley Award wins: 2011, 2012, 2015 x2, 2017 (5 wins). Locus Award wins: 2017, 2018 (2 wins). British Fantasy Award win (year TBC). World Fantasy Award win (year TBC).
Julie Dillon.confidence_note: BFA and WFA wins confirmed from profiles but exact years to verify

Todd Lockwood.credentials: Chesley Award wins: 1996, 1997, 1998, 1999, 2001, 2003, 2004 (Artistic Achievement), 2007, 2008, 2013, 2014, 2016. 12 Chesley wins total.
Todd Lockwood.notes: Painted APs listed as coming soon. 73 MTG cards.

Volkan Baga.credentials: Chesley Award win 2009 (Best Gaming-Related Illustration, Stoic Angel, Shards of Alara). National Portrait Gallery London — selected exhibition.
Volkan Baga.notes: 213 MTG cards. Direct inquiry only. 645 Instagram followers — low competition. Traditional oils. NPG London selection independently qualifies.
Volkan Baga.confidence_note: NPG London selection to verify at npg.org.uk

Dan Dos Santos.credentials: Chesley Award wins: 2007 (Best Cover Paperback, Moon Called), 2009 (Best Product, Hellboy 2), 2014 (Best Cover Magazine, Fables #136). Spectrum Gold Medal. Jack Gaughan Award.
Dan Dos Santos.notes: 18 MTG cards only. Stoneforge Mystic (WWK) the key card. Hugo win NOT confirmed from primary sources.
Dan Dos Santos.confidence_note: Hugo win removed — unverified. Chesley wins confirmed from ASFA source.

Tyler Jacobson.credentials: Chesley Award win 2016 (Best Cover Illustration Paperback, Beyond the Pool of Stars by Howard Andrew Jones).
Tyler Jacobson.notes: 67 MTG cards. Confirmed SCGCon LA October 9-11 2026. AFR cards are priority targets.

Lucas Graciano.credentials: Chesley Award wins: 2010 (Best Gaming-Related, Silverwing Legends of Norrath), 2011 (Best Gaming-Related, Amorphous Drake), 2014 (Best Gaming-Related, Last Stand of Thorin Oakenshield). 3 Chesley wins.
Lucas Graciano.notes: Custom oil painted APs available. Contact: lucasgraciano@yahoo.com. 59 MTG cards.

Raoul Vitale.credentials: Chesley Award wins: 2010 (Best Color Work Unpublished, Unrequited), 2012 (Best Monochrome Unpublished, The Yeti), 2013 (Best Monochrome Unpublished, Last of His Kind), 2015 (Best Cover Paperback, Nebula Awards Showcase 2014). Multiple Chesley wins.
Raoul Vitale.notes: 126 MTG cards. Traditional oils. One foil Aether Vial AP remaining at $750 + $600 painted back. Top card: Aether Vial Kaladesh Inventions ($120).

Justin Sweet.credentials: Chesley Award win 2006 (Best Product Illustration, The Lion the Witch and the Wardrobe production design).
Justin Sweet.notes: 66 MTG cards. Traditional oils. No dedicated AP store found. Direct outreach needed.

Matthew Stewart.credentials: Chesley Award wins: 2010 (Best Cover Illustration Hardcover, Valley of Shadows) + 2010 (Best Product Illustration, Battle Under the Mountain). 2 Chesley wins.
Matthew Stewart.notes: 128 MTG cards. Top card: Ulamog the Infinite Gyre ($28).

Peter Mohrbacher.credentials: Chesley Award wins: 2015 (Best Gaming-Related Illustration, Pharika God of Affliction), 2016 (Best Interior Illustration, Angelarium: Book of Emanations). 2 Chesley wins.
Peter Mohrbacher.notes: Stopped MTG 2014. Works exclusively on Angelarium. Painted AP pathway unclear. 45 MTG cards.

Jason Chan.credentials: Chesley Award win 2011 (Best Cover Illustration Paperback, Geist by Phillippa Ballantine).
Jason Chan.notes: 75 MTG cards. Digital-first. Signs via MountainMage.

Marc Simonetti.credentials: Chesley Award win 2018 (Best Hardback Book Cover, The Sword of Shannara by Terry Brooks).
Marc Simonetti.notes: Only 7 MTG cards — very limited AP pool. Archangel of Thune ($12) top card.

Jon Foster.credentials: Chesley Award win 2019 (Best Cover Illustration Hardcover, I Met a Traveller in an Antique Land by Connie Willis).
Jon Foster.notes: 19 MTG cards. AP availability needs research.

Mitchell Malloy.credentials: Chesley Award win 2019 (Best Gaming-Related Illustration, Frantic Search, Ultimate Masters).
Mitchell Malloy.notes: 12 MTG cards. AP availability needs research.

Gabor Szikszai.credentials: Chesley Award win 2006 (Best Gaming-Related Illustration, Blazing Archon, with Zoltan Boros).
Gabor Szikszai.notes: 115 MTG cards. Digital-primary. Painted AP availability unclear.

Zoltan Boros.credentials: Chesley Award win 2006 (Best Gaming-Related Illustration, Blazing Archon, with Gabor Szikszai).
Zoltan Boros.notes: 199 MTG cards. Digital-primary. Painted AP availability unclear.

John Avon.notes: 208 MTG cards. Nominations only. Does not meet framework threshold.

Terese Nielsen.credentials: Hugo and Chesley nominations only — no wins confirmed.

Seb McKinnon.notes: 93 MTG cards. Popular community artist but no framework credentials.

Christopher Rush.credentials: No major award wins — but OG MTG Lane case could be argued (Black Lotus artist).
Christopher Rush.notes: Died 2016. Creator of Black Lotus. 123 MTG cards. OG MTG Lane exception arguable.
